title: Recover missing acceptance criteria after user clarification
user_request: Make invoice export friendlier for accountants.

harness_gap.description: The initial request is intentionally vague; the worker proceeds without converting a user clarification into durable acceptance criteria.

decision_signals: supervisor attribution to missing spec capture
decision contract for durable acceptance criteria

intervention_trace.summary: The user clarified that friendly means CSV columns must be stable, dates must be ISO formatted, and totals must include tax.
evidence: user clarification: stable CSV columns, ISO dates, tax-inclusive totals
first patch changed labels but did not satisfy accounting import rules

decision_contract.hypothesis: The worker failed because the harness lacked a durable path for turning clarification into acceptance criteria.
decision_contract.expected_effect: Adding acceptance criteria and tests should let a replaying worker implement accounting-friendly export semantics without repeated clarification.
decision_contract.falsification_check: Replay asks for the same clarification again or produces label-only changes.

permissions: supervisor may add acceptance criteria and tests
worker may edit exporter code during replay
worker must not rewrite unrelated CLI commands
information_boundaries: clarification content becomes visible acceptance criteria

replay_protocol.replay: run worker from the original user_request with captured acceptance criteria available in the harness
success_criteria: acceptance criteria are represented in a durable harness artifact
the final implementation is reached without repeating the clarification

notes: Seed card for benchmark design; executable fixture still needs to be built.
